element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental mechanic taught through opening missions

element_introduction_order.element: Food resource cap and unit limits

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Individual unit focusing and ability usage
Economic constraint forcing tactical unit discipline

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Campaign selection (Humans, Orcs, Night Elves, Undead)

typical_relief_triggers: Successful base establishment and resource flow
Safe zones with hero units protected
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy unit sightings through fog of war
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Asymmetric waves: players manage economy and scouting during calm periods, then execute defensive or offensive operations when threats emerge; campaign missions provide scripted tension escalation

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Off-map areas hidden by fog of war until explored; terrain features (mountains, rivers) create exploration boundaries
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Neutral creep camps and hidden gold mines reward exploration beyond main quest paths; varied map topography hides strategic positioning opportunities

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like progression introducing individual factions and basic mechanics through scripted campaign missions; initial objectives are straightforward resource gathering and base defense
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of upkeep penalties and complex unit composition decisions; enemy AI becomes more aggressive; multiple simultaneous objectives require divided attention
difficulty_curve.late_game: Full faction capabilities unlocked; advanced unit combinations and hero abilities required; large-scale battles against coordinated enemy forces; campaign culminates in faction-specific final missions against Burning Legion/Scourge threat
notable_spikes: First major enemy assault waves in early campaign
Introduction of upkeep economic penalty system
Endgame faction-specific boss encounters and World Tree defense sequences

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player campaign structured through four playable factions (Humans, Orcs, Night Elves, Undead) with interconnected story; each faction told in sequence revealing different perspectives on central conflict
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Human and Orc campaigns introduce basic mechanics; Night Elf and Undead campaigns reveal endgame threat

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Map Exploration with Fog of War
notable_level_patterns.description: Campaign maps start with hidden terrain; players must scout to reveal map layout, locate resources, and discover enemy positions; terrain features (rivers, mountains, cliffs) create natural pathfinding constraints
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All campaign maps utilize fog of war discovery mechanic

notable_level_patterns.description: Players balance three resources (gold, lumber, food) with upkeep penalty system that punishes unit spam; strategic settlement placement determines resource access and vulnerability to raids
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Persistent mechanic across all campaign missions

notable_level_patterns.description: Campaign missions frequently center on protecting or moving hero units to specific locations; hero units serve as both powerful combat units and narrative anchors
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Campaign maps show hero unit portrait in UI corner; quest markers indicate hero movement objectives
